tool_args.label: Jamaica Capacity Improvements - Phase 1
tool_args.raw_text: JCI Phase 1 is funded by the 2010-2014 Capital Program. The project includes the construction of a new platform and associated infrastructure required to support future shuttle service between Jamaica Station and the Atlantic Branch Stations.

payload.description: Construction of new Platform F at Jamaica Station for Brooklyn-to-Jamaica shuttle service. Project is approximately 92% complete. Budget $321.7M, original $301.7M. Substantial completion forecast Q4 2022.

evidence_refs.source_quote: JCI Phase 1 is funded by the 2010-2014 Capital Program
PROJECT STATUS Original Forecast JCI Phase 1 Program - Substantial Completion Oct 2021 Q4 2022 Budget $301.7 M $321.7 M
original project Budget of $301.6M remains with an Estimate at Completion (EAC) of $321.7M

tool_args.label: Queens Interlocking
tool_args.raw_text: Queens Interlocking is a critical junction point at the west end of the Main Line Third Track and Hempstead Branch, which is also the location of the new Elmont Station.

payload.description: Modification of interlocking to support new Elmont Station and increased traffic from East Side Access and Main Line Expansion. Budget $155M. Approximately 16% complete. Substantial completion forecast January 2025.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Queens Interlocking is a critical junction point at the west end of the Main Line Third Track and Hempstead Branch
PROJECT STATUS Current Forecast Substantial Completion Jan 2025 Jan 2025 Budget $155.0 M $155.0 M The project is approximately 16% complete.

tool_args.label: Harmon Shop Replacement Phase V Stage 2
tool_args.raw_text: This Design-Build project will replace MNR's 100+ year old maintenance facility in Croton-on-Hudson. The Harmon shop, along with its supporting facilities, are the largest electric car maintenance facility for MNR rolling stock.

payload.project_name: Harmon Shop Replacement Phase V Stage 2
payload.description: Demolition and reconstruction of 250,000 sq ft Main Shop and storeroom at Croton-on-Hudson, replacement of heavy equipment. Budget $439.6M. Approximately 75% complete. Substantial completion forecast June 2023.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Harmon Shop Phase 5 Stage 2 This Design-Build project will replace MNR's 100+ year old maintenance facility in Croton-on-Hudson.
PROJECT STATUS Current Forecast Substantial Completion April 2023 June 2023 Project Budget $439.6 M $439.6 M The project is approximately 75% complete.

tool_args.label: Park Avenue Viaduct Replacement Phase 1
tool_args.raw_text: The Park Avenue Viaduct (PAV) replacement project will replace the existing 130-year-old railroad viaduct from East 115th Street to East 123rd Street, along Park Avenue in Harlem.

payload.description: Replacement of 130-year-old viaduct from E 115th St to E 123rd St along Park Ave in Harlem. Carries 4 third-rail powered tracks, approximately 750 trains per day. RFP issued August 30, 2022. Construction expected Q3 2023, completion 2028.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Park Avenue Viaduct (PAV) replacement project will replace the existing 130-year-old railroad viaduct from East 115th Street to East 123rd Street, along Park Avenue in Harlem.
Procurement for this design-build project is underway with an RFP issued on August 30th, with an award anticipated before the end of this year.
On site construction work is expected to commence in Q3 of 2023 with completion in 2028.

tool_args.label: JCI Phase 2 Hall Interlocking Expansion
tool_args.raw_text: Hall Interlocking Expansion comprises Stage 3 and Stage 4 (Hall) and includes the eastward extension of existing E-Yard tracks via the construction of a new bridge over 150th Street. Ultimately, this project will enable the creation of two additional parallel routes within Hall Interlocking (new tracks E2 and E3) which is critical to the overall master plan of JCI Phase 2.

payload.claim_text: Hall Interlocking Expansion will enable the creation of two additional parallel routes within Hall Interlocking (new tracks E2 and E3) which is critical to the overall master plan of JCI Phase 2

tool_args.label: JCI Phase 2 Hall Interlocking Expansion
tool_args.raw_text: The Hall Interlocking Expansion comprises Stage 3 and Stage 4 (Hall) and includes the eastward extension of existing E-Yard tracks via the construction of a new bridge over 150th Street. The work also involves replacing/modifying existing retaining walls, modifications to existing Guy Brewer Blvd bridge structures, associated track/3rd rail modifications, and SOGR. Ultimately, this project will enable the creation of two additional parallel routes within Hall Interlocking (new tracks E2 and E3) which is critical to the overall master plan of JCI Phase 2.

payload.claim_text: Hall Interlocking Expansion comprises Stage 3 and Stage 4 (Hall) and includes the eastward extension of existing E-Yard tracks via the construction of a new bridge over 150th Street. The project will enable the creation of two additional parallel routes within Hall Interlocking (new tracks E2 and E3) critical to the overall JCI Phase 2 master plan.

payload.description: Hall Interlocking Expansion extends E-Yard tracks eastward over new bridge on 150th Street, creating two additional parallel routes
